title: Grounds for Disciplinary Action Against Foreign Employers
content: The usual complaints against foreign employers that serve as grounds for disciplinary action include: 1) Default on contractual obligations to the migrant worker and/or its Philippine agent; 2) Gross violation of laws, rules, and regulations on overseas employment; 3) Gross negligence leading to serious injury, illness, or death of the worker; 4) Grave misconduct; 5) Conviction of an offense involving moral turpitude; 6) Any other cases analogous to the foregoing.

metadata.source_file: 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs Primer.pdf
